description: The two exact-fold paths fold EVERY op regardless of batching, in deliberate contrast to the flush-granular edge path. Explicit merge() calls and Topic-driven merges are exact because the CALLER decides how many ops exist; delivery through a dependency edge is flush-granular because the GRAPH decides. This fixture runs both paths against the same accumulator in the same batch so the contrast is structural rather than a matter of comparing two files: three explicit calls and one settled cone deliver 3 + 1 folds, not 4 and not 2. A binding that unified the two paths -- making explicit calls flush-granular, or the edge path per-write -- passes each of the single-path fixtures in isolation and fails only here. That is the point: merge_per_settled_cone_not_per_write.json and merge_folds_synchronously_in_batch.json each pin one path, and a binding can satisfy both with one rule applied inconsistently. Contract: #lzmergefeed.

expect.note: baseline fold of a=0 at effect creation

THE assertion: 1 (baseline) + 3 explicit calls (exact, one per call) + 1 edge delivery (flush-granular, one per settled cone) = 5. Unifying the paths gives 3 (all flush-granular) or 7 (all per-write).
0 + 100 + 200 + 300 + 2 -- every explicit op, and only the SETTLED value of a. The intermediate a=1 never reaches the accumulator.
